document.purpose: Master index, reading order, and execution directives
Vision, transformation pillars, success metrics, and SOTA positioning
Bend feature to Axon integration mapping with feasibility and impact scores
Target architecture, compiler pipeline extensions, and data flow contracts
8 workstreams, dependency graph, acceptance gates, and execution prompts
Epic-level executable backlog with stories, dependencies, and definition-of-done
Claude-optimized execution protocol, context budgets, and phase handoff schema
Ready-to-run prompt templates per workstream, 9-field Hybrid-CO-STAR format
Context lifecycle, handoff packets, compression policy, and token budget rules
Snapshot testing strategy, golden-run baselines, stress and regression suites
Release-readiness scorecard with hard stop/go criteria per phase
Risk identification, mitigation strategies, and residual risk tracking
Performance benchmark framework for tensor ops, fusion, and parallelism
Architectural decision records for key integration choices
XSD schema and CI quality enforcement for plan documents

recommendedReadOrder.step: Read 01 (strategy) and 06 (execution playbook) first to align on vision and execution behavior.
Read 02 (capability matrix) and 03 (architecture) to understand what we're building and where it fits.
Read 04 (workstreams) and 05 (backlog) to see the full work breakdown and dependency graph.
Use 07 (prompts) and 08 (context governance) continuously during execution for quality and stability.
Use 09 (validation), 10 (gates), and 11 (traceability) as non-negotiable quality and release gates.
Consult 12 (risks), 13 (benchmarks), and 14 (decisions) as living references throughout.

executionDirectives.directive: Axon's existing 1,034 tests are the mandatory regression baseline — no plan phase may reduce test count.
All code changes must compile with zero warnings and pass full cargo test before marking any story complete.
Context budget per execution phase: max 80K tokens. Use handoff packets between phases.
Each workstream must produce at least one snapshot/golden-run test as evidence of correctness.
Bend ideas are adapted to Axon's paradigm (imperative, ownership-based, LLVM-targeting) — never copy Bend's implementation directly.
GPU codegen features (Phases C/E) are deferred until MLIR pipeline is operational — plan them but do not block on them.

nonNegotiables.rule: No backward-incompatible syntax changes without full test migration.
No new compiler pass may increase compile time by more than 15% without documented justification.
Every new MIR transform pass must have unit tests AND integration tests.
Operator fusion correctness must be verified by numerical equivalence tests (epsilon < 1e-6).
All plan XML documents must validate against the XSD schema before merge.
